title: CS50 2D - Introduction
summary: CS50 2D is Harvard's introduction to 2D game development, exploring design and mechanics of classic games like Pong, Flappy Bird, Breakout, and Super Mario Bros. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.
writeup: This is the course trailer for CS 50's Introduction to 2D Game Development, taught by Colton Ogden under the CS 50 umbrella. It lays out the syllabus arc: starting from classic titles like Pong, Breakout, Flappy Bird, and Super Mario Brothers, students work through the core mechanics behind interactive games. The through-line is building from primitive black-and-white shapes up to physics, side-scrolling, and top-down games, all in Lua with the Love2D framework.
key_points: The course uses Lua and the Love2D framework as its entire technical stack, requiring only prior programming experience in any language as a prerequisite.

The curriculum spans a historical arc of game genres: Pong, Breakout, Legend of Zelda, Super Mario Brothers, Flappy Bird, Match 3 (Candy Crush), Angry Birds, and Pokemon serve as the implementation targets.
Core computer science topics covered include the game loop, event handling, state machines, interpolation, rudimentary and advanced physics, side-scrolling, and top-down game architecture.
